title: Human Induced Pluripotent Stem Cell (hiPSC) Culture and Passaging Protocol
author: Elena Coccia
materials: Passage hiPSCs when they reach 70-80% confluency.
Aspirate the old medium from the plates.
Gently pipette to ensure single-cell suspension.

steps.title: Prepare Geltrex-coated plates and StemFlex medium for hiPSC culture
steps.instruction: Prepare Geltrex-coated plates and StemFlex medium for hiPSC culture.

steps.title: Dilute Geltrex in cold DMEM/F12 to...
steps.instruction: Dilute Geltrex in cold DMEM/F12 to a final concentration of 1:

steps.title: Add 3 mL of diluted Geltrex...
steps.instruction: Add 3 mL of diluted Geltrex to each 100 mm plate.

steps.title: Incubate at 37 °C for 1 hour to allow coating
steps.instruction: Incubate at 37 °C for 1 hour to allow coating.
1. Thaw StemFlex medium at room temperature. 2. Supplement with 10 µM Y-27632.

steps.title: Rinse cells with 3 mL of...
steps.instruction: Rinse cells with 3 mL of PBS without Ca2+ and Mg2+.

steps.title: Add 3mL of Accutase to each plate
steps.instruction: Add 3mL of Accutase to each plate.

steps.title: Incubate at RT for 3 minutes until cells are dissociated
steps.instruction: Incubate at RT for 3 minutes until cells are dissociated.

steps.title: Pipette the cells into 10ml of StemFlex + Y-27632
steps.instruction: Pipette the cells into 10ml of StemFlex + Y-27632.

steps.title: Centrifuge the cell suspension at 300 g for 5 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the cell suspension at 300 g for 5 minutes.

steps.title: Discard the supernatant and resuspend the...
steps.instruction: Discard the supernatant and resuspend the cell pellet in 1 mL of StemFlex medium containing 10 µM Y-

steps.title: Transfer the cell suspension to new Geltrex-coated plates
steps.instruction: Transfer the cell suspension to new Geltrex-coated plates.
